ui.title: Blog
ui.lede: Clear, accurate answers to the questions our patients ask most.

midcab.title: What is MIDCAB? A guide to minimally invasive coronary bypass
midcab.excerpt: How a 5–8 cm incision below the left breast replaces the traditional sternotomy — and which patients are good candidates.
midcab.imageAlt: Gloved hands of surgeons performing minimally invasive cardiac surgery

off-pump-vs-on-pump.title: Off-pump vs. on-pump bypass: which approach, when?
off-pump-vs-on-pump.excerpt: We compare the trade-offs of the heart-lung machine versus operating on a beating heart.
off-pump-vs-on-pump.imageAlt: Cardiac surgical team working in a modern operating theatre

bypass-recovery.title: Recovering from bypass: the first 12 weeks
bypass-recovery.excerpt: What to do, what to avoid, and when to return to work and to social life after bypass surgery.
bypass-recovery.imageAlt: Patient walking through a hospital corridor during bypass recovery

classic-vs-minimal-bypass.title: Classic open bypass vs. minimally invasive: which fits which patient?
classic-vs-minimal-bypass.excerpt: Evidence-based outcomes, anatomy, and patient profile — we walk through how the choice is actually made.
classic-vs-minimal-bypass.imageAlt: Cardiac surgical team in a planning huddle

tavi.title: What is TAVI? Aortic valve replacement without open surgery
tavi.excerpt: A new-generation aortic valve, delivered through a groin artery — designed for older or higher-risk patients.
tavi.imageAlt: Anatomical heart model with medical instruments

sutureless-aortic.title: Sutureless aortic valves: a faster, suture-free biological valve
sutureless-aortic.excerpt: By eliminating suturing time, this approach shortens cross-clamp time and offers an alternative for higher-risk surgical candidates.
sutureless-aortic.imageAlt: Close-up of a prosthetic aortic valve

mitral-repair-vs-replacement.title: Mitral valve: repair or replace?
mitral-repair-vs-replacement.excerpt: The long-term advantages of preserving your own valve, and the situations where replacement becomes the right call.
mitral-repair-vs-replacement.imageAlt: Medical illustration of heart valve anatomy

valve-disease-signs.title: Heart valve disease: symptoms you shouldn’t ignore
valve-disease-signs.excerpt: Breathlessness, palpitations, ankle swelling — which symptoms suggest valve disease, and when should you see a specialist?
valve-disease-signs.imageAlt: Doctor listening to a patient’s heart with a stethoscope

aortic-aneurysm-guide.title: Aortic aneurysm: from diagnosis to treatment
aortic-aneurysm-guide.excerpt: A silent condition that can become life-threatening. Screening, follow-up, and the surgical decision.

aortic-dissection-emergency.title: Aortic dissection: an emergency where minutes matter
aortic-dissection-emergency.excerpt: What a Type A dissection is, why it requires surgery within hours, and how our 24/7 team is structured around it.

bentall-vs-david.title: Bentall vs. David: choosing between two root procedures
bentall-vs-david.excerpt: Should the aortic valve be preserved, or replaced together with the root? We make the decision based on anatomy and long-term goals.
bentall-vs-david.imageAlt: Anatomical model of the aortic root

living-with-aneurysm.title: Living with an aneurysm: surveillance, lifestyle, control
living-with-aneurysm.excerpt: Follow-up intervals, blood-pressure management, and daily-life recommendations for aneurysms that don’t yet need surgery.
living-with-aneurysm.imageAlt: Person walking along a coastal path

atrial-myxoma.title: Atrial myxoma: the most common benign cardiac tumour
atrial-myxoma.excerpt: Often diagnosed by accident. How it’s found, why it must be removed.

maze-procedure.title: The MAZE procedure: surgically ending atrial fibrillation
maze-procedure.excerpt: When fibrillation is long-standing or accompanies other cardiac surgery, surgical ablation can restore sinus rhythm.

pericardiectomy.title: Pericardiectomy: relieving the constrained heart
pericardiectomy.excerpt: When constrictive pericarditis impairs cardiac filling, removing the pericardium delivers significant relief from breathlessness and oedema.
pericardiectomy.imageAlt: Chest imaging of heart and lungs

when-cardiac-surgery.title: When is cardiac surgery necessary? A patient’s roadmap
when-cardiac-surgery.excerpt: We clarify the line between conditions managed with medication and those where surgery shouldn’t be delayed.
when-cardiac-surgery.imageAlt: Cardiac surgeon talking with a patient
